variant: v10c — editorial long-read in dark mode (friend of v7c)

visual_quality: Newsreader serif for hero h1 and section headlines, Work Sans for chrome — editorial pairing
Drop caps on first paragraph of each long-read section (indigo→mango gradient, 4 lines tall)
Pull quotes drawn from body, large serif, left rule (alternating Mango / Indigo)
Two-column long-form on programs (Pattern B) with sticky marginalia
Single-column long-read on LDVP (~720px) with Atlantic-feature feel
Quieter aurora than v7c (0.18 vs 0.35 indigo intensity); tighter grid (72px vs 56px)
Date stamps in muted serif italics, stat lines as inline prose-friendly blocks
brand_consistency: Work Sans 300/400/500/600/700 used for nav, eyebrows, body, CTAs, marginalia
Newsreader serif used only for editorial typography (display headlines, italics, drop caps, pull quotes)
All gradients use --grad-text (Indigo→Mango), preserving v7c's gradient text accent
Step chips, eyebrows, breadcrumbs, footers all preserved from v7c
content_accuracy: 20/20 nuggets landed on at least one expected page
8/8 depth pages have Open Questions block (1-3 items each, sourced from IA roster)
10/10 homepage trail teasers are verbatim scoped claims (≥8 words, no forbidden CTAs)
Per-LDVP word counts: Verify 2191, Learn 1537, Deliver 1758, Pay 1496
Per-program word counts: CHC 2531, KMC 1417, ECD 1398, Readers 1051
responsiveness: Two-column long-form collapses to single column at 960px; marginalia stops sticking
Editorial pillar/audience grids collapse to vertical stacks with re-flowed borders
Drop caps reduce from 4.6em to 4em on mobile
Trail items collapse from 3-col to single column with arrows hidden at 560px
Mobile nav (hamburger + slide-in panel) inherited from v7c, tested in CSS
code_quality: Single styles.css (~43KB, ~900 lines) — extends v7c DNA; no external CSS frameworks
Inline JS only for mobile nav toggle — same pattern as v7c
Google Fonts (Newsreader + Work Sans) the only external resource
All images have alt text; viewport meta on every page
depth_sharpness: Top 3 nuggets by score (verbatim phrasing landed across expected pages):
